criteria: eBPF program written in C and compiled to BPF bytecode using clang with -target bpf and BTF debug info (-g flag)
Program attaches to kprobe on do_sys_openat2 using libbpf skeleton with SEC("kprobe/do_sys_openat2") macro
CO-RE (BPF_CORE_READ) used for accessing kernel struct fields including open_how->flags and open_how->mode
Data passed from kernel to userspace via BPF_MAP_TYPE_RINGBUF with bpf_ringbuf_reserve/submit pattern
Userspace consumer polls ring buffer using ring_buffer__poll() and displays PID, comm, timestamp, dfd, filename, and flags
At least one verifier rejection intentionally triggered with captured error log and explanation of why it was rejected
bpf_probe_read_kernel_str() used for reading kernel filename pointer safely without direct dereference
Program runs with CAP_BPF/CAP_SYS_ADMIN capability and capability requirements documented in code comments
Ring buffer graceful degradation implemented: NULL check on bpf_ringbuf_reserve with early return on failure
Build system generates vmlinux.h from /sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux and creates skeleton header using bpftool
Entry kprobe records bpf_ktime_get_ns timestamp in BPF hash map keyed by thread ID (lower 32 bits of bpf_get_current_pid_tgid)
Exit kretprobe retrieves start timestamp from hash map, computes duration, and deletes the hash map entry
Latency is converted to log2 histogram bucket index using 64 - __builtin_clzll(latency_us) with special handling for zero latency
Histogram uses BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY with 64 buckets indexed by syscall number, where bucket N covers 2^N to 2^(N+1)-1 microseconds
Userspace periodically reads histogram map via bpf_map_lookup_elem and displays formatted output with bucket ranges, counts, percentages, and ASCII bar charts
Multiple syscalls (read, write, openat minimum) are traced with separate histograms per syscall number
Hash map uses BPF_ANY flag for update and gracefully handles -E2BIG return when map is full by dropping the measurement
Configuration map allows enabling/disabling individual syscalls at runtime without reloading the eBPF program
All timestamps use bpf_ktime_get_ns (monotonic) rather than wall-clock time to handle NTP adjustments and suspend/resume correctly
